metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Machine Identity & Communications

content:
MIRA garage reference — Garage demo conveyor: identity and communications.

This is the MIRA bench/garage conveyor used for the 'text your factory' demo. It is a single belt conveyor driven by a 3-phase motor through a VFD, controlled by a small Allen-Bradley PLC.

**Controller (PLC):** Allen-Bradley Micro820, catalog 2080-LC20-20QBB (also seen as 2080-LC20-20QBBN), firmware 14. IP address 192.168.1.100 on the bench LAN. Programmed with Connected Components Workbench (CCW 22). Active firmware project: Conv_Simple (a.k.a. Conv_Simple_1.5+).
**Drive (VFD):** AutomationDirect GS10 DURApulse AC drive.
**Drive link:** PLC is Modbus RTU MASTER over the embedded RS-485 port (CCW Channel 2). GS10 is slave node 1. Serial settings 9600 baud, 8 data / No parity / 1 stop bit (8N1). The PLC polls the GS10 status block every 500 ms.
**Northbound:** the PLC also runs a Modbus TCP SLAVE on Ethernet port 502 so Ignition / scripts / dashboards can read unpacked values. It also speaks EtherNet/IP (port 44818) for CCW programming.
**Upstream demo path:** Phone -> Telegram -> VPS (Jarvis, 100.68.120.99) -> PLC laptop (192.168.1.50 / Tailscale 100.72.2.99) -> Ignition 8.3.4 -> Micro820. The PLC laptop must keep Tailscale --accept-routes=false, or peer pi-factory hijacks the 192.168.1.0/24 route and PLC traffic dies.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Sequence of Operations & State Machine

content:
MIRA garage reference — Garage conveyor sequence of operations.

The conveyor runs a small state machine in the PLC. The state integer conv_state is published on Modbus TCP holding register HR:114 (1-based).

**States (conv_state):**
- 0 = IDLE — motor stopped, waiting for a start command / start pushbutton.
- 1 = STARTING — start accepted; contactor pulled in, VFD commanded to RUN, short start delay timer running.
- 2 = RUNNING — motor turning at the commanded direction/speed.
- 3 = STOPPING — stop accepted; VFD commanded STOP, stop delay timer running before contactor drops.
- 4 = FAULT — a fault condition latched (e-stop, direction fault, or VFD comm loss).

**Start:** a run command (start pushbutton DI, or Ignition write of run_cmd / vfd_cmd_word over Modbus) is accepted only when the system is healthy: e-stop healthy, no e-stop wiring fault, no direction fault, and the photo-eye soft-stop is not latched. On accept: IDLE -> STARTING -> RUNNING; the safety contactor DO_02 energizes, the green light DO_00 turns on, and the VFD command word is set to FWD+RUN (18) or REV+RUN (20).
**Stop:** removing the run command (or any stop condition) drives RUNNING -> STOPPING -> IDLE; vfd_cmd_word := 1 (STOP) and the contactor drops after the stop timer.
**Direction:** a 2-wire selector decodes to dir_fwd (DI_00) / dir_rev (DI_01). Both wires active at once => dir_fault and the machine goes to FAULT. Changing direction while running also forces a fault/stop.

error_code (HR:106) encodes the active fault: 7 = e-stop, 8 = direction fault, 9 = VFD comm loss.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Dual-Channel E-stop Safety

content:
MIRA garage reference — Garage conveyor dual-channel e-stop safety circuit.

The emergency stop uses TWO redundant, opposite-sense channels into the PLC so a single broken wire is detected, not silently ignored.

**Channel A — DI_02 (_IO_EM_DI_02), Normally-Closed:** healthy / not-pressed state = TRUE. Goes FALSE (open) when the e-stop is pressed or its wire breaks.
**Channel B — DI_03 (_IO_EM_DI_03), Normally-Open:** healthy / not-pressed state = FALSE. Goes TRUE (closed) when the e-stop is pressed.

**Healthy logic:** the two channels must be OPPOSITES (XOR). Healthy = DI_02 TRUE and DI_03 FALSE. E-stop pressed = DI_02 FALSE and DI_03 TRUE.
**Wiring fault:** if BOTH channels read the SAME state (both TRUE or both FALSE), that is impossible for a good circuit, so the PLC raises estop_wiring_fault (Modbus coil 10) — this indicates an open wire, short, or stuck contact, NOT a real e-stop press.

**Effect:** when e-stop is active OR a wiring fault is present, the safety contactor DO_02 (Q1) is de-energized, the motor cannot run, and the machine goes to FAULT (error_code 7). The contactor energizes ONLY when the e-stop is healthy and there is no wiring fault. Published flags: e_stop_active = coil 6, estop_wiring_fault = coil 10.

Diagnostic tip: 'motor won't start, e-stop not pressed' + estop_wiring_fault TRUE => check the e-stop wiring (one of the two channels is open or stuck), not the e-stop button itself.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Photo-Eye Latching Soft-Stop

content:
MIRA garage reference — Garage conveyor photo-eye (PE-01) latching soft-stop.

A through-beam photo-eye (PE-01) watches the conveyor near the discharge/infeed. The digital input DI_05 reads the beam (beam clear vs beam broken).

**Behavior — LATCHING soft-stop:** when the photo-eye beam is BROKEN (an item blocks it / a jam), the PLC LATCHES a soft stop. This sets pe_latched = TRUE. A soft stop means the PLC commands the VFD to STOP (vfd_cmd_word := 1) but, unlike an e-stop, it does NOT drop the safety contactor — it is a controlled stop, not an emergency trip.

**It STAYS latched** even after the beam clears. The conveyor will not restart on its own. **Re-arm / clear the latch by pressing Start (run command) with the beam CLEAR.** If the beam is still broken when Start is pressed, the latch will not clear.

**Run permit:** the PLC computes vfd_run_permit = DO_02 (contactor) AND e_stop_ok AND NOT pe_latched. The drive is only allowed to run when vfd_run_permit is TRUE. So a latched photo-eye (pe_latched TRUE) forces vfd_run_permit FALSE and the motor stays stopped.

Published flags (Ignition tags): pe_latched (Boolean), vfd_run_permit (Boolean).

Diagnostic tip: 'conveyor stopped by itself and won't restart, e-stop fine' => check pe_latched. If TRUE, clear the blockage at the photo-eye, confirm the beam is clear, then press Start to re-arm.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — GS10 Command Words & Control Registers

content:
MIRA garage reference — GS10 DURApulse command words and control registers (the values the PLC writes to run the motor).

**Control / command registers (write, Modbus FC06):**
- 0x2000 (dec 8192) Control Command — bit field (run/stop + direction).
- 0x2001 (dec 8193) Frequency Setpoint — unsigned, value/10 = Hz (4000 = 400.0 Hz max).
- 0x2002 (dec 8194) Control Code 2 — write bit1 (value 2) to RESET a fault.

**Control Command (0x2000) bit field:** bits 0-1 = run/stop (01=STOP, 10=RUN, 11=JOG+RUN); bits 3-4 = direction (01=FWD, 10=REV, 11=toggle).

**The three command words the conveyor actually uses (vfd_cmd_word):**
| Command   | Decimal | Hex    | Bits set                |
|-----------|---------|--------|-------------------------|
| STOP      | 1       | 0x0001 | Stop (bit0)             |
| FWD + RUN | 18      | 0x0012 | Run (bit1) + FWD (bit3) |
| REV + RUN | 20      | 0x0014 | Run (bit1) + REV (bit4) |

So to start the motor forward the PLC writes 18 to 0x2000; reverse writes 20; stop writes 1. To clear a drive fault, write 2 to 0x2002 (or press STOP/RESET on the GS10 keypad).

GS10 control-source parameters that must be set for Modbus control: P00.21 = 2 (Run command source = RS-485 Modbus). Frequency via register 0x2001 needs P00.20 set appropriately. Note: as of the 2026-05-28 read-only build the PLC did not yet forward writes to the GS10 — control writes are a later phase; the vfd_cmd_word register on the TCP slave is an echo until then.

WARNING (AB MSG_MODBUS offset): the Micro820 SUBTRACTS 1 from the configured Addr before sending. To hit wire 0x2000 the ST sets Addr := 8193 conceptually — verify against the integration guide; the guide lists the register Addr values used by the ST blocks (write_cmd Addr 8192, write_freq Addr 8193).

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — GS10 Fault Code Table

content:
MIRA garage reference — GS10 DURApulse VFD fault codes seen on the garage conveyor (vfd_fault_code, read from wire 0x2100). 0 = no fault. Each fault halts the drive and latches until cleared via the keypad STOP/RESET key or by writing 2 to register 0x2002.

| Code | Display | Name | Likely cause | Fix |
|------|---------|------|--------------|-----|
| 0  | -    | No fault             | normal                                  | -                                              |
| 4  | GFF  | Ground fault         | Motor/cable insulation short to ground  | Check / megger motor wiring                     |
| 12 | Lvd  | Low voltage on decel | DC bus dipped during deceleration       | Check supply; extend decel time                 |
| 21 | oL   | Overload             | Motor overloaded / jam                   | Reduce load; check mechanics & motor sizing     |
| 49 | EF   | External fault       | External fault input active             | Check external fault wiring                     |
| 54 | CE1  | Comm command error   | Invalid Modbus function code            | Use FC 03/06/16; do not use FC23                |
| 55 | CE2  | Comm address error   | Register out of range                    | Use valid GS10 addresses (0x2000-2 / 0x2100-F)  |
| 56 | CE3  | Comm data error      | Data value out of range                  | Check the value written                         |
| 57 | CE4  | Comm slave error     | Internal slave fault                     | Power-cycle the VFD                             |
| 58 | CE10 | Comm timeout         | No Modbus packets received (master dead) | Check baud/wiring/P09.03; check PLC RS-485 link |
| F30.x | F30 | Comm fault latch    | Comm loss detected repeatedly            | Press STOP/RESET on keypad                      |

Clearing a fault: (1) press STOP/RESET on the GS10 keypad, or (2) write value 2 to register 0x2002 (dec 8194) over Modbus, or (3) if it will not clear, power-cycle the VFD.

Note the CE-class codes (CE1/CE2/CE4/CE10) are communications faults — they point at the Modbus link between the Micro820 and the GS10, not at the motor. CE10 in particular means the GS10 stopped hearing the PLC.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — GS10 Status Word & Monitor Registers

content:
MIRA garage reference — GS10 status word bits and status-monitor registers (the read-only feedback block the PLC polls).

**Status word — vfd_status_word, wire register 0x2101 (dec 8449):** the low two bits encode run state:
- bits 1-0 = 00 -> STOPPED
- bits 1-0 = 01 -> DECELERATING
- bits 1-0 = 10 -> STANDBY / ready
- bits 1-0 = 11 -> RUNNING

**Fault/error in status monitor 1 — wire 0x2100 (dec 8448):** high byte = warning, low byte = error code; the low byte is the vfd_fault_code (0 = no fault). See the GS10 fault code table.

**Status monitor registers polled (read, FC03):**
| Wire | Dec  | Meaning             | Raw scale (GS10 native) |
|------|------|---------------------|-------------------------|
| 0x2100 | 8448 | Status monitor 1 (warning/error) | byte fields |
| 0x2101 | 8449 | Status monitor 2 (op status bits)| bit field   |
| 0x2102 | 8450 | Frequency command   | Hz x10 |
| 0x2103 | 8451 | Output frequency    | Hz x10 |
| 0x2104 | 8452 | Output current      | A x10  |
| 0x2105 | 8453 | DC bus voltage      | V x1   |
| 0x2106 | 8454 | Output voltage      | V x10  |

Note: these are the GS10's NATIVE raw scales. The PLC re-scales them before publishing on its own Modbus TCP slave and OPC tags (see the tag-scaling chunk: published values are Hz x100, A x100, V x10).

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — GS10 Serial / Modbus Comms Config

content:
MIRA garage reference — GS10 RS-485 / Modbus comms configuration that makes the PLC<->VFD link work on the garage bench.

**Working link (verified):** Modbus RTU, GS10 slave node 1, 9600 baud, 8N1, PLC = master on its embedded RS-485 port (CCW Channel 2). The PLC polls the GS10 status block every ~500 ms.

**KEY GOTCHA — P09.09 Response Delay:** the GS10 default response delay (~2 ms) is TOO FAST for the Micro820's RS-485 transceiver to switch from transmit back to receive; the PLC misses the reply and the link looks dead. FIX: set P09.09 = 10 ms (or more). This single change is what brought the long-silent link alive (20/20 clean round-trips).

**GS10 P09 communication parameters:**
- P09.00 = 1  (comm address / slave node; must match PLC Node=1)
- P09.01 = 96 (baud; 96 = 9600. The OEM guide's default-template shows 38.4k, but the BENCH runs 9600)
- P09.02 = 0  (comm fault treatment: warn and keep running — avoids nuisance trips during commissioning)
- P09.03 = 5.0 (timeout seconds; detects a dead master)
- P09.04 = protocol/framing code. 12 = 8N1 RTU, 13 = 8N2 RTU. The bench link is 8N1, so P09.04 = 12 to match the PLC's 8N1. (The OEM template lists 13/8N2 as the Micro820 default; always re-triage and match whatever the PLC serial port is actually set to.)
- P09.09 = 10 ms response delay (see gotcha above)
- P09.31 = 0 (internal comm protocol = Modbus 485)

**PLC serial port (CCW):** driver Modbus RTU, Master, RS-485, 9600 baud, 8 data bits, No parity, 1 stop bit, no handshake.

Power-cycle the GS10 after changing any P09.xx parameter. If the link goes silent: re-run scripts/gs10_triage.py first, confirm P09.09 >= 10 ms, and check RS-485 A/B polarity (bench cable is known to be polarity-reversed end-to-end).

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Ignition VFD Tag Semantics & Scaling

content:
MIRA garage reference — Ignition VFD tag semantics and raw->engineering scaling for the garage conveyor. These OPC tags read the PLC by CIP global-variable name from device [MIRA_PLC]; NO Modbus map is needed for Ignition (it uses the Micro800 driver).

**Scaling rule of thumb (PLC-published values):** Frequency = raw / 100 (Hz). Current = raw / 100 (A). Voltage = raw / 10 (V). Example: vfd_frequency 6000 -> 60.00 Hz; vfd_dc_bus 3270 -> 327.0 V (~327 V is the normal idle DC bus on a ~240 VAC-fed drive).

**Tags:**
- vfd_frequency (Int) — GS10 output frequency, raw Hz x100. Divide by 100.
- vfd_current (Int) — GS10 output current, raw A x100. Divide by 100.
- vfd_dc_bus (Int) — GS10 DC bus voltage, raw V x10. Divide by 10. ~327 idle.
- vfd_freq_sp (Int) — commanded frequency setpoint written to the GS10, raw Hz x100 (3000 = 30.00 Hz).
- vfd_cmd_word (Int) — GS10 command echo: 1 = STOP, 18 = FWD+RUN, 20 = REV+RUN.
- vfd_status_word (Int) — GS10 status (bits 1-0: 00=stopped, 01=decel, 10=standby, 11=running).
- vfd_fault_code (Int) — GS10 fault/error code, 0 = no fault (see fault table).
- vfd_comm_ok (Bool) — Modbus comm watchdog; TRUE = PLC<->VFD link healthy. **This is the TRUST GATE.**
- vfd_run_permit (Bool) — PLC-computed: contactor DO_02 AND e_stop_ok AND NOT pe_latched. TRUE = drive allowed to run.
- pe_latched (Bool) — photo-eye soft-stop latch; TRUE = a beam-break latched a stop (clear by pressing Start with beam clear).

**Trust gate:** if vfd_comm_ok is FALSE, the VFD numeric tags are STALE (last good read). Dashboards must alarm/mask the VFD tiles rather than show frozen numbers as if live.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Modbus TCP Slave Register/Coil Map

content:
MIRA garage reference — Garage conveyor Modbus TCP slave map (what dashboards/scripts read FROM the PLC). PLC is the Modbus TCP SLAVE at 192.168.1.100:502, unit/slave id 1. Addresses are **1-based on the wire**; pymodbus is 0-based, so SUBTRACT 1. Big-endian, 16-bit WORD, no word swap. Verified working 2026-05-28 (Conv_Simple_1.5).

**Coils (FC01 read / FC05 write):**
| 1-based | pymodbus offset | Variable | Meaning |
|---------|-----------------|----------|---------|
| 1  | 0  | motor_running       | Conveyor motor commanded on |
| 2  | 1  | conveyor_running    | Conveyor running |
| 3  | 2  | fault_alarm         | Fault alarm latched |
| 4  | 3  | vfd_comm_ok         | RTU link to GS10 healthy (TRUST GATE) |
| 5  | 4  | system_ready        | System ready |
| 6  | 5  | e_stop_active       | E-stop pressed |
| 7  | 6  | dir_fwd             | Direction forward decoded |
| 8  | 7  | dir_rev             | Direction reverse decoded |
| 9  | 8  | heartbeat           | Scan-oscillator liveness |
| 10 | 9  | estop_wiring_fault  | Dual-channel XOR violation (open/short wire) |
| 11 | 10 | dir_fault           | Both direction wires active |
| 12..16 | 11..15 | _IO_EM_DI_00.._IO_EM_DI_04 | Raw DI mirror (Sel FWD/REV, EstopNC/NO, Fault-Reset PB) |
| 17..20 | 16..19 | _IO_EM_DO_00.._IO_EM_DO_03 | Raw DO mirror (GreenLED, RedLED, Contactor Q1, PB-Run LED) |

**Holding registers (FC03 read / FC06 / FC16) — all WORD, integer-scaled:**
| 1-based | pymodbus offset | Variable | Raw -> engineering |
|---------|-----------------|----------|--------------------|
| 106 | 105 | error_code     | fault code (7=estop, 8=dir_fault, 9=vfd_comm) |
| 107 | 106 | vfd_frequency  | Output Hz x100 (6000 = 60.00 Hz) |
| 108 | 107 | vfd_current    | Output A x100 |
| 109 | 108 | vfd_voltage    | Output V x10 |
| 110 | 109 | vfd_dc_bus     | DC bus V x10 (~3270 = 327 V idle) |
| 111 | 110 | item_count     | sensor rising-edge count |
| 112 | 111 | uptime_seconds | seconds since last download |
| 113 | 112 | conveyor_speed_cmd | speed command from Ignition |
| 114 | 113 | conv_state     | state (0=IDLE 1=START 2=RUN 3=STOP 4=FAULT) |
| 115 | 114 | vfd_cmd_word   | GS10 cmd echo (1=STOP, 18=FWD+RUN, 20=REV+RUN) |
| 116 | 115 | vfd_freq_setpoint | setpoint x0.1 Hz (300 = 30.0 Hz) |

Authoritative XML: Conv_Simple_1.5/Controller/Controller/MbSrvConf.xml.

GOTCHAS: (1) Don't write coils 1-11 — they are internal ladder state. Coils 12-20 are read-only raw I/O mirrors. (2) HR values are integer-scaled; engineering-unit tags are NOT exposed — scaling is the dashboard's job. (3) vfd_comm_ok FALSE => HR values are stale; mask the dashboard. (4) dir_off, heartbeat-as-local, and a few LOCAL vars are not mappable from inside a POU.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage Conveyor — Key PLC Global Variables

content:
MIRA garage reference — Garage conveyor key PLC global variables (name, what it means, where exposed). From the variable manifest. Field devices map to inputs/outputs; computed flags carry the safety and state logic.

**Physical inputs (DI):**
- _IO_EM_DI_00 — Direction selector FWD wire (alias 'Direction FWD'). Coil 12.
- _IO_EM_DI_01 — Direction selector REV wire. Coil 13.
- _IO_EM_DI_02 — E-stop channel A, Normally-Closed, healthy = TRUE. Coil 14.
- _IO_EM_DI_03 — E-stop channel B, Normally-Open, healthy = FALSE. Coil 15.
- _IO_EM_DI_04 — Fault Reset / Start pushbutton. Coil 16.
- _IO_EM_DI_05 — Photo-eye PE-01 / Sensor 1 (drives the latching soft-stop).
- _IO_EM_DI_06 — SensorEnd (item counting on rising edge).

**Physical outputs (DO):**
- _IO_EM_DO_00 — Green LED 'Running'. Coil 17.
- _IO_EM_DO_01 — Red LED 'Fault'. Coil 18.
- _IO_EM_DO_02 — Safety contactor Q1 (drive enable). Energizes only when e-stop healthy + no wiring fault. Coil 19.
- _IO_EM_DO_03 — PB Run LED. Coil 20.

**Computed / state flags:**
- motor_running (coil 1), conveyor_running (coil 2), fault_alarm (coil 3), system_ready (coil 5).
- e_stop_active (coil 6), estop_wiring_fault (coil 10), dir_fwd (7), dir_rev (8), dir_fault (11), heartbeat (9).
- vfd_comm_ok (coil 4) — VFD link health / trust gate. vfd_comm_err — internal error flag.
- conv_state (HR:114), error_code (HR:106 — 7=estop, 8=dir_fault, 9=vfd_comm).

**VFD feedback / command globals:** vfd_frequency (HR:107), vfd_current (HR:108), vfd_voltage (HR:109), vfd_dc_bus (HR:110), vfd_cmd_word (HR:115; 1/18/20), vfd_freq_setpoint (HR:116). Diagnostic counters vfd_poll_count / vfd_err_count and MSG_MODBUS result codes read_vfd_errorid / write_cmd_errorid / write_freq_errorid are LOCAL (not on the wire) — view them in the CCW Variable Monitor.

Naming note: ST source and manifest must agree on names (Conv_Simple uses alias-in-code 'Pattern B'); a mismatch (DI_* vs _IO_EM_DI_*) once caused a silent live-bridge failure (CRA-257).
